nodes.label: AI as tone checker for professional communication
nodes.grounding_span: I ask for criticism on the overall tone and feel

nodes.label: AI as research starting point
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human creativity
nodes.grounding_span: using AI to help with research as well as finding similar works

nodes.label: AI as research accelerator
nodes.label_negative: AI as demotivating or praise-only tool
nodes.grounding_span: having the assistant on hand to provide feedback has been a motivator

nodes.label: AI as proofreading assistant
nodes.grounding_span: using it for the proofreading/editing aspect is a big area I could see using way more as this is the biggest expense

nodes.label: AI as second brain and personal assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as generic tool without personalization
nodes.grounding_span: the more I use a model the more it will learn my writing and desires as a creative so it will become more and more of a 'second brain'

nodes.label: Market saturation from AI-generated content
nodes.grounding_span: the market can become filled with works by people who have used AI to write a whole story without much actual input

nodes.label: AI as plagiarism risk
nodes.label_negative: Clear ethical boundaries for AI training
nodes.grounding_span: concerns about the work being used to train models, and this should be looked at carefully as it is such a fine line between plagiarism and inspiration

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I am the driving force for the creative decisions

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it really made the process flow better

nodes.label: Market access and fair competition
nodes.grounding_span: standing out is like a needle in a haystack

nodes.label: Ethical and responsible AI use
nodes.grounding_span: this should be looked at carefully as it is such a fine line between plagiarism and inspiration

nodes.label: Positive appreciation for AI as research catalyst
nodes.grounding_span: having that instant feedback on something which, as a writer, you often don't get feedback until you've finished the whole thing which can take a long, long time!! As such, this provided me with a great motivation to keep going

nodes.label: Concern about market flooding by AI-generated works
nodes.grounding_span: the market can become filled with works by people who have used AI to write a whole story without much actual input from themselves

nodes.label: Concern about plagiarism in AI training
nodes.grounding_span: concerns about the work being used to train models, and this should be looked at carefully as it is such a fine line between plagiarism and inspiration

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: the more I use a model the more it will learn my writing and desires as a creative so it will become more and more of a 'second brain'

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I am careful to specify I don't want suggestions for the story, as I feel this could influence my writing

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: I use the previous conversation to continue this so the model's know my aim
